question:
Will A Resistance of Witches (Morgan Ryan) win Readers’ Favorite Fantasy in the 2025 Goodreads Choice Awards?

Description: The Goodreads Choice Awards are presented annually by Goodreads.com. For the 2025 Goodreads Choice Awards, the winners will be announced on December 4, 2025.

This market will resolve according to the listed book that wins the Readers' Favorite Fantasy award in the 2025 Goodreads Choice Awards.

If, for any reason, no winner is declared by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the case of a tie for the winner, this market will resolve in favor of the listed book that comes first in alphabetical order.

The resolution source will be the Goodreads website (https://www.goodreads.com/choiceawards/best-books-2025); however, other credible reporting may be used.

evidences.title: Goodreads choice awards | Bookshelf Fantasies
evidences.content: Honestly, I’m not even sure what the point is anymore. Over the years, the number of categories has decreased. The write-in option for round one is gone. And now, there are only two rounds. It kind of feels like Goodreads is continuing the awards because they have to, but they don’t actually care at all about making it make sense for readers. How do they determine what books get nominated for round one? No idea! And I can’t find the answer, other than that dates of publication are specified for eligibility. I’m not necessarily mad at the winners — but given how limited the choices were to begin with, I’m not sure how meaningful any of this is. For what it’s worth, here are the Goodreads Choice Award winners for 2024: And in text format: [...] I’m so glad that I finally read The Hate U Give, and will be pushing it on my family and friends. evidences.content: Cannonball Read 18 Sticking It to Cancer One Book at a Time | Log in 1. Follow us on Facebook 2. Follow us on Instagram 3. Follow us on Bluesky 4. Follow us on Goodreads 5. RSS Feeds # Witches and Nazis and Ghosts – Oh My A Resistance of Witches by Morgan Ryan by Jen K Leave a Comment The author definitely has potential and I’d be curious to see what else she does but I’d say the novel is a solid 3 – really interesting ideas but there were plot points that could have been fleshed out more to make it stronger. Other themes and plot were very much conventional (mother-daughter conflicts, the old ways vs the new ways, and I don’t think anyone is going to be surprised by the twist) but within that, there are some very emotional and moving scenes. [...] The novel really could have been more accurately titled Resistance of Witch – this won’t be a surprise from the book blurb but the majority of the novel focuses on the events after the witches decide to stop supporting the war effort and Lydia goes on a one-witch mission to France. The problem with this is that I really don’t think Ryan did a good enough job here convincing us why the witches wouldn’t want to support the fight against...

evidences.title: Goodreads Choice Awards: Should we even care at this point?
evidences.content: I’m not necessarily mad at the winners — but given how limited the choices were to begin with, I’m not sure how meaningful any of this is. For what it’s worth, here are the Goodreads Choice Award winners for 2024: And in text format: [...] # Bookshelf Fantasies ## A Life Amidst Books # Menu # Goodreads Choice Awards: Should we even care at this point? Goodreads has already announced the winners of the 2024 Goodreads Choice Awards… and as far as I’m concerned, the announcement has landed with a big thud. Honestly, I’m not even sure what the point is anymore. Over the years, the number of categories has decreased. The write-in option for round one is gone. And now, there are only two rounds. It kind of feels like Goodreads is continuing the awards because they have to, but they don’t actually care at all about making it make sense for readers. How do they determine what books get nominated for round one? No idea! And I can’t find the answer, other than that dates of publication are specified for eligibility. evidences.title: Book Review: A Resistance of Witches | Amy Rose Davis
evidences.content: Skip to content Amy Rose Davis Amy Rose Davis # Book Review: A Resistance of Witches 4 Comments / November 18, 2025 Pop quiz! (Don’t worry, it’s multiple choice.) As you read this, I am: A) On the road, heading to Arizona; B) Watching movers load all our stuff into a truck; or C) Frantically packing the last of our belongings before movers arrive. I actually have no idea. The moving company gave us a window of dates, which meant we couldn’t really nail down our own travel plans until they were confirmed, which means that as I write this, I’m still wondering exactly how this is all going to come together. I may be a pantser in my writing, but in real life, I’m a planner and a plotter, and this ambiguity definitely makes me twitchy as hell. via GIPHY [...] ## Will I Read More of This Author? This is Morgan Ryan’s first novel, and it’s an excellent debut. I will definitely check out her future works. ## What’s Next On My List? Next on my list is–still–that pesky interstate move. I’m finding it tough to think much further ahead than… well, tomorrow… But I am not leaving y’all high and dry! evidences.title: my goodreads choice awards book recommendations! (nominees + winners)
evidences.content: Fargo. I know people say this is a romance, but I think the romance is more of a subplot in the story. We're following figure skaters and they are like world famous figure skaters. It's the 10-year anniversary of their last skate together and there's a documentary coming out about them and like their their figure skating history. And that was like the best part of this book because the structure of it is so interesting. You get transcript of the documentary and the people that are talking on it are like other figure skaters. They're coaches. They're people that are like within the real story telling their opinions and their like tell all of what happened with our figure skaters, our main character. And then you get the actual real story of our main characters and how they got into figure [...] really, really fast-paced. They're a really good time. And I feel like the author did a great job of the fantasy and the high stakeness of it along with the romance. We're following Rune in Heartless Hunter and she is an undercover witch vigilante. And then our witch hunter is Gideon and he is tasked with killing the crimson moth which is like her vigilante name. So she's undercover trying...
